publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-02-12 18:38:47 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Hoshang Merchant is known as India's first openly gay poet. He was educated in Mumbai and in the USA, and has traveled an taught widely, now residing in Hyderabad. Merchant describes his writing as 'therapeutic.'.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hoshang Merchant papers, #7861. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Collection contains papers, correspondence, clippings, and photos that document Hoshang Merchant's poetry, family, travels, and personal life.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Publications removed from the collection and cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.

list.head: Separated Publications
list.item: Ed. Hoshang Merchant. Yaraana: Gay Writing from India, 1999
Nishat, Jameela. A Poem Slumbers in My Heart, 1999

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary entries may be found in other series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry may be found in other series.

Friendship Poems to 17 Year Old Boy Rasheed, Neighbor

Last Poems for my Dead Love Yunees, Unpublished
A Tale of Two Yusufs - Unpublished Manuscript
A Light Within the Body Unpublished Manuscript
Les Paradis Ne Sont Artificircs Manuscript
Tagore, Neruda, Cesaire: A View from the South

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Prose may be found in other series.

Palestine Manuscripts and Parts of Autobiography

scopecontent.p: Correspondence may be found in other series.

Empire of the Mind: What did the British do for Indian Education
